title: Cadence retrospective entry
description: Structured JSON form of a single retrospective finding, drafted by the Reviewer subagent and used as the basis for FRAMEWORK_CHANGELOG.md entries.

feature.description: Name of the feature run this retrospective covers

what_happened.description: One-line description of the observed issue

auto_catchable.description: Whether the issue could be caught mechanically

auto_method.description: If auto_catchable, what mechanism would catch it (lint, schema, AST check)

rule_reference.description: ADR-NNNN or PATTERNS.md §X if rule_existed was true

landed_in.description: Commit SHA, PR number, or other reference where the approved fix landed

near_miss.description: True if this is a gap that turned out fine, false if a real issue caught

violation_sample.description: Structured form of the offending code. Consumed by tool/emit_rule.py for Layer 3 boundary-class findings: lets the framework auto-generate a fixture + boundary rule and verify the rule actually fires on this exact sample before the finding is recorded as landed.

kind.description: Which auto-emitter handles this sample. Only 'boundary-rule' is wired in v0.3.0-rc.1; future emitters (lint-rule, schema-rule) will add enum values.

language.description: File extension hint for the generated fixture. The checker is language-agnostic, but the fixture file needs the right extension to be scanned.

where.description: Glob pattern for the file location that must not perform this import (matches cadence.yaml boundaries[].where). Example: 'src/features/**'.

import_line.description: The exact offending import line as it appeared in the source. The emitter writes this verbatim into the generated fixture.

forbidden_pattern.description: Glob the import resolves to that the rule must forbid (matches cadence.yaml boundaries[].forbidden[]). Example: 'src/data/sources/**'.

reason.description: Why this boundary exists. Becomes the cadence.yaml rule's reason field; surfaces in the checker's error output.
